title: Seahorse Assay for Measuring Oxygen Consumption Rate in iPSC-Derived Neurons
author: Tzu-Chieh Huang

steps.title: Seed 1
steps.instruction: Seed 1.65x10^5^ Day 5 iPSC-derived glutamatergic neurons (iGLUTs) into each well of a XF24 microplate and continue differentiation until Day 21 for the mitochondrial stress test.

steps.title: Prior to the assay, remove most...
steps.instruction: Prior to the assay, remove most of the culture medium, leaving only 50 µL per well, and add 1000 µL pre-warmed Seahorse XF DMEM supplemented with 25mM glucose and 0.23mM pyruvate.

steps.title: Incubate the Seahorse plate at 37...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Seahorse plate at 37 °C in a non-CO2 incubator for 1 hour.

steps.title: After the 1-hour incubation, replace the...
steps.instruction: After the 1-hour incubation, replace the assay medium with 500 µL of fresh assay medium for the Seahorse assay.

steps.title: Measure the plate using Seahorse XFe24...
steps.instruction: Measure the plate using Seahorse XFe24 Analyzer with the standard Mito Stress Test program. The program includes three sequential injections of mitochondrial inhibitors: 1.5 µM oligomycin, 1.5 µM carbonyl cyanide 4-(trifluoromethoxy) phenylhydrazone, and a mixture of 0.5 µM rotenone and 0.5 µM antimycin A.

steps.title: After recording the oxygen consumption rate,...
steps.instruction: After recording the oxygen consumption rate, lyse the cells with 30 µL of M-PER™ Mammalian Protein Extraction Reagent containing cOmplete™ Mini Protease Inhibitor Cocktail and PhosSTOP™, according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Measure total protein concentration using Pierce™...
steps.instruction: Measure total protein concentration using Pierce™ Dilution-Free™ Rapid Gold BCA Protein Assay.

steps.title: Normalize oxygen consumption rates to total protein content
steps.instruction: Normalize oxygen consumption rates to total protein content.
